This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # Handle object\n258\t    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n259\t        rows = []\n260\t        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n261\t            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | createFallbackCard).\n274\t\n275\t    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n276\t    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n277\t    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n278\t\n279\t    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n280\t\n281\t    Args:\n282\t        summary: Optional result summary\n283\t        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n284\t        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n285\t\n286\t    Returns:\n287\t        HTML string for the fallback card\n288\t    \"\"\"\n289\t    try:\n290\t        # Build fallback card HTML\n291\t        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n292\t\n293\t        # Header with icon and title\n294\t        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n295\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span quote=False)}</p>')\n302\t\n303\t        # Key/value grid from data\n304\t        if data:\n305\t            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n306\t            if rows:\n307\t                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n308\t                for key, value in rows:\n309\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n310\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n311\t                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n312\t\n313\t        # Urgency badge\n314\t        if urgency:\n315\t            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n316\t\n317\t        card_parts.append('</div>')\n318\t\n319\t        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n320\t\n321\t    except Exception as e:\n322\t        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n323\t        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n324\t        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n325\t        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n326\t\n327\t\n328\t#  \"\"\"\n345\t    global _renderer\n346\t    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n347\t        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n348\t    return no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":349,"startLine":1,"totalLines":349}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_311560e8bd5b4b39aa03782f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSSE broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n3\t\n4\tManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n5\t\"\"\"\n6\t\n7\timport asyncio\n8\timport json\n9\timport logging\n10\timport os\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime\n13\tfrom typing import Dict\n14\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n15\t\n16\t# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n40\t# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n41\t# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n42\t# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n43\t# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n44\t# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n45\t# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n46\t_DROP = object()\n47\t\n48\t\n49\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n50\t\n51\t\n52\t@dataclass\n53\tclass      event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n142\t        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n\nManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\n# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n\n    Attributes:\n        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n        data: The event payload data\n        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n    \"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    rendered_html: str | None = None\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n                    continue\n\n                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                payload = dict(event.data)\n                if event.rendered_html is not None:\n                    payload[\"rendered_html\"] = event.rendered_html\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, payload)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in cleanup loop: {e}\")\n\n\n# Global broadcaster instance\n_broadcaster: SSEBroadcaster | None = None\n\n\ndef get_broadcaster() -> SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global SSE broadcaster.\"\"\"\n    global _broadcaster\n    if _broadcaster is None:\n        _broadcaster = SSEBroadcaster()\n    return _broadcaster\n\n\n# Event types\nclass EventType:\n    \"\"\"SSE event types.\"\"\"\n\n    # Connection lifecycle\n    CONNECTED = \"connected\"\n    DISCONNECT = \"disconnect\"\n    HEARTBEAT = \"heartbeat\"\n\n    # Result events\n    RESULT_CREATED = \"result_created\"\n    RESULT_UPDATED = \"result_updated\"\n\n    # Component events (Phase 2)\n    COMPONENT_UPDATED = \"component_updated\"\n\n    # Intent events\n    INTENT_PENDING = \"intent_pending\"\n    INTENT_DISPATCHED = \"intent_dispatched\"\n    INTENT_RESOLVED = \"intent_resolved\"\n\n    # Topic events\n    TOPIC_CREATED = \"topic_created\"\n    TOPIC_UPDATED = \"topic_updated\"\n    TOPIC_STALE = \"topic_stale\"\n\n    # Workload events\n    WORKLOAD_SUMMARY = \"workload_summary\"\n    EXCEPTION_RAISED = \"exception_raised\"\n\n    # Bead events\n    BEAD_CLOSED = \"bead_closed\"\n    BEAD_FAILED = \"bead_failed\"\n\n    # Circuit breaker events\n    TASK_STUCK = \"task_stuck\"\n    TASK_FAILED = \"task_failed\"\n\n\nasync def broadcast_result(\n    result: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    rendered_html: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"\n    Broadcast a result to relevant surfaces.\n\n    Args:\n        result: The result data payload\n        session_id: The session ID to target\n        target_surface_id: Optional specific surface to target\n        rendered_html: Optional pre-rendered HTML for canvas injection\n\n    Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.RESULT_CREATED,\n        data=result,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=target_surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_intent_update(\n    intent: dict,\n    session_id: str,\n    event_type: str = EventType.INTENT_RESOLVED,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast an intent status update.\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=event_type,\n        data=intent,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n\n\nasync def broadcast_workload_summary(\n    session_id: str,\n    summary: dict,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Broadcast a workload summary (for reconnection).\"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    event = SSEEvent(\n        event_type=EventType.WORKLOAD_SUMMARY,\n        data=summary,\n        target_session_id=session_id,\n        target_surface_id=surface_id,\n    )\n    return await broadcaster.broadcast(event)\n","numLines":394,"startLine":1,"totalLines":394}}}

Returns \"\" on failure\n131\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n132\t        \"\"\"\n133\t        try:\n134\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n135\t        except Exception as e:\n136\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n137\t            return \"\"\n138\t\n139\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n140\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n141\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n142\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n143\t        if urgency_prompt:\n144\t            system_prompt = (\n145\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n146\t            )\n147\t        return system_prompt\n148\t\n149\t    async def classify_utterance(\n150\t        self,\n151\t        utterance: str,\n152\t        session_id: str,\n153\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n154\t        \"\"\"\n155\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n156\t\n157\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n158\t\n159\t        Args:\n160\t            utterance: The user utterance\n161\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n162\t\n163\t        Returns:\n164\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n165\t        \"\"\"\n166\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n167\t\n168\t        # Get recent intents for context\n175\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n176\t                if recent_intents:\n177\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n178\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n179\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n180\t        except Exception as e:\n181\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n182\t\n183\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n184\t\n185\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n186\t\n187\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n188\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n189\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n190\t\n191\t        try:\n192\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n193\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n194\t                user_message=user_message,\n195\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n196\t                max_tokens=2048,\n197\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n198\t            )\n199\t\n200\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n201\t            raw = response.strip()\n202\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n203\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n204\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n205\t\n206\t            # Parse JSON response\n207\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n208\t            classifications = []\n209\t\n210\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n211\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n212\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n213\t                try:\n214\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n215\t                except ValueError:\n216\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n217\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n218\t\n219\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n220\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n221\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n222\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n223\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n224\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n225\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n226\t                )\n227\t                classifications.append(classification)\n228\t\n229\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n230\t            return classifications\n231\t\n232\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n233\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n234\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n235\t            return [\n236\t                IntentClassification(\n237\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n238\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n239\t                    confidence=0.5,\n240\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n241\t                )\n242\t            ]\n243\t        except Exception as e:\n244\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n245\t            raise\n246\t\n247\t    async def route_utterance(\n248\t        self,\n249\t        utterance: str,\n250\t        utterance_id: str,\n251\t        session_id: str,\n252\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n253\t        \"\"\"\n254\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n255\t\n256\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n257\t\n258\t        Args:\n259\t            utterance: The user utterance\n260\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n261\t            session_id: Session ID\n262\t\n263\t        Returns:\n264\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n265\t        \"\"\"\n266\t        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n267\t        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n268\t        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n269\t        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n270\t        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n271\t        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n272\t\n273\t        routed_intents = []\n274\t        for classification in classifications:\n275\t            # Create intent ID\n276\t            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n277\t\n278\t            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n279\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n280\t                classification=classification,\n281\t                session_id=session_id,\n282\t                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n283\t                router_ms=router_ms,\n284\t            )\n285\t            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n286\t\n287\t        return routed_intents\n288\t\n289\t    async def process_intent(\n290\t        self,\n291\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n292\t    ) -> dict:\n293\t        \"\"\"\n294\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n295\t\n296\t        Args:\n297\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n298\t\n299\t        Returns:\n300\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n301\t        \"\"\"\n302\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n303\t\n304\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n305\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n306\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n307\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n308\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n309\t        # the dispatch itself.\n310\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n311\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n312\t\n313\t        try:\n314\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n315\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n316\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n317\t            else:\n318\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n319\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n320\t        except Exception:\n321\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n322\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n323\t            raise\n324\t\n325\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n326\t        return result\n327\t\n328\t    async def _persist_timings(\n329\t        self,\n330\t        intent_id: str,\n331\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n332\t    ) -> None:\n333\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n334\t        try:\n335\t            store = await self._get_store()\n336\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n337\t        except Exception as e:\n338\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n339\t\n340\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n341\t        self,\n342\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n343\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n344\t    ) -> dict:\n345\t        \"\"\"\n346\t        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n347\t\n348\t        Args:\n349\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n350\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n351\t                fetch_total_ms, and                ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n378\t                    host_alias = entry.host\n379\t                    location = entry.display_path\n380\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n381\t                else:\n382\t                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n383\t\n384\t   )\n408\t\n409\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n410\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n411\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n412\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n413\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n414\t\n415\t            def           )\n491\t\n492\t            result_id = await store.create_result(\n493\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n494\t                topic_id=topic_id,\n495\t intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n        # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n        # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n        classify_start = time.monotonic()\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n        router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                router_ms=router_ms,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings:
