timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n\n            def _on_first_source(_source, _result) -> None:\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_first_source)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=None,  # Not populated in current classification\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=None,  # Not populated in current classification\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n                fence_context = {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = await self._check_fence_for_bead(result.bead_id)\n                if fence_context:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Detected immediate fence for bead {result.bead_id} \"\n                        f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                    )\n                    # Return stuck card instead of pending card\n                    return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n                \"message\": f\"Escalated to bead {result.bead_id}\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalation failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to escalate to bead\",\n            }\n\n\n# Global router instance\n_router: Optional[IntentRouter] = None\n\n\ndef get_router(store=None) -> Generate utterance ID if not provided\n437\t    if not utterance_id:\n438\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n439\t\n440\t    # Create utterance record\n441\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n442\t\n443\t    # Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n487\t    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n488\t    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n489\t    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n490\t\n491\t    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n492\t    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n493\t    \"\"\"\n494\t    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n495\t    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n496\t    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n497\t    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n498\t\n499\t    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n500\t\n501\t    store = await get_store()\n502\t    router = get_intent_router(store)\n503\t\n504\t    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n505\t    if not utterance_id:\n506\t        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n507\t\n508\t    # Create utterance record\n509\t    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n510\t\n511\t    # Route the utterance\n512\t    try:\n513\t        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n514\t            utterance=utterance,\n515\t            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n516\t            session_id=session_id,\n517\t        )\n518\t\n519\t        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n520\t        intent_tasks = []\n521\t        intent_ids = []\n522\t\n523\t        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n524\t            # Create intent record in store\n525\t            classification = routed_intent.classification\n526\t            await store.create_intent(\n527\t                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n528\t                session_id=session_id,\n529\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n530\t                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n531\t            )\n532\t            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n533\t\n534\t            # Create task for parallel processing\n535\t            task = asyncio.create_task(\n536\t                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n537\t                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n538\t            )\n539\t            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n540\t\n541\t        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n542\t        async def stream_results():\n543\t            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n544\t            results = []\n545\t\n546\t            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n547\t                try:\n548\t                    result = await task\n549\t                    results.append(result)\n550\t\n551\t                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n552\t                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n553\t                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n554\t                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n555\t                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n556\t                        sse_data = {\n557\t                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n558\t                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n559\t                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n560\t                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n561\t                        }\n562\t                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n563\t                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n564\t                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n565\t                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n566\t                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n567\t                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n568\t\n569\t                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n570\t                            SSEEvent(\n571\t                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n572\t                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n573\t                                data=sse_data,\n574\t                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n575\t                            )\n576\t                        )\n577\t                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n578\t                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n579\t                        try:\n580\t                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n581\t                                intent_id,\n582\t                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n583\t                            )\n584\t                        except Exception as te:\n585\t                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n586\t\n587\t                except Exception as e:\n588\t                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n589\t                    results.append({\n590\t                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n591\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n592\t                        \"error\": str(e),\n593\t                    })\n594\t\n595\t            return results\n596\t\n597\t        # Start parallel processing in background\n598\t        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n599\t\n600\t        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n601\t        return {\n602\t            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n603\t            \"session_id\": session_id,\n604\t            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n605\t            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n606\t            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n607\t            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n608\t        }\n609\t\n610\t    except Exception as e:\n611\t        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n612\t        return JSONResponse(\n613\t            status_code=500,\n614\t            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n615\t        )\n616\t\n617\t\n618\t@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\n619\tasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n620\t    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n621\t\n622\t    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n623\t    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n624\t    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n625\t    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n626\t    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n627\t    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n628\t    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n629\t    stay NULL.\n630\t\n631\t    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n632\t    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n633\t    \"\"\"\n634\t    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n635\t    if not intent_id:\n636\t        return JSONResponse(\n637\t            status_code=400,\n638\t            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n639\t        )\n640\t\n641\t    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n642\t    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n643\t        value = request.get(name)\n644\t        if value is not None:\n645\t row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n651\t        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n652\t        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n653\t    except Exception as e:\n654\t        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n655\t        return JSONResponse(\n656\t            status_code=500,\n657\t list(fields.keys())}\n661\t\n662\t\n663\t@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\n664\tasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n665\t    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per DB_PATH.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n    Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Initialize session store\n    _store = session_store_get_store(DB_PATH)\n    await logger.info(\"SSE broadcaster started\")\n\n    # Initialize topic manager\n    _topic_manager = TopicManager(_store)\n    logger.info(\"Topic manager initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize surface router\n    _surface_router = SurfaceRouter(_store)\n    logger.info(\"Surface router initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize component library\n    _component_library = get_library()\n    logger.info(\"Component library initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize hot-reload manager\n    _reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n    logger.info(\"Hot-reload manager initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize feedback processor\n    _feedback_processor = get_feedback_processor()\n    logger.info(\"Feedback processor initialized\")\n\n    # Initialize ambient monitor (Phase 3)\n    _ambient_monitor = get_ambient_monitor()\n    await _ambient_monitor.start()\n    logger.info(\"Ambient monitor started\")\n\n    # Initialize context warmer (Phase 3)\n    _context_warmer = telegram_fallback = get_telegram_fallback()\n        bridge_available = await telegram_fallback.check_bridge_available()\n        if bridge_available:\n            logger.info(f\"Telegram bridge reachable at {telegram_fallback.bridge_url}\")\n        else:\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Telegram bridge unreachable at {telegram_fallback.bridge_url}. \"\n                f\"Telegram fallback will not be available.\"\n            )\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Failed to check Telegram bridge reachability: {e}\")\n\n    # Initialize bead watcher\n    try:\n        _bead_watcher = BeadWatcher(_store, _surface_router)\n        await _bead_watcher.start()\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Bead watcher failed to start: {e}\")\n        _bead_watcher = None\n\n    logger.info(\"aide-de-camp ready\")\n    yield\n\n    # Shutdown\n    logger.info(\"Shutting down aide-de-camp...\")\n    stop_background_refresh()\n    if _bead_watcher:\n        await _bead_watcher.stop()\n    if _background_processor:\n        await _background_processor.stop()\n    if _context_warmer:\n        await _context_warmer.stop()\n    if _ambient_monitor:\n        await _ambient_monitor.stop()\n    if _broadcaster:\n        await _broadcaster.stop()\n    if _component_library:\n        _component_library.close()\n    if _store:\n        await _store.close()\n    logger.info(\"aide-de-camp shutdown complete\")\n\n\napp = FastAPI(title=\"ADC (aide-de-camp)\", version=read_version(), lifespan=lifespan)\n\n# Include test router\napp.include_router(test_router, prefix=\"/api/v1\", tags=[\"test\"])\n\n\nasync def get_store():\n    \"\"\"Get or initialize the global session store.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        _store = session_store_get_store(DB_PATH)\n        await _store.initialize()\n    return _store\n\n\n@app.get(\"/health\")\nasync def health_check():\n    \"\"\"Health check endpoint.\n\n    Returns overall service status and watcher liveness block (alive, last_tick_at,\n    tick_count, interval). Watcher alive is true only while the task is running\n    AND last_tick_at is within 2x the poll interval. If _bead_watcher is None\n    (failed start), watcher.alive is false.\n    \"\"\"\n    response = {\n        \"status\": \"ok\",\n        \"service\": \"adc-voice\",\n    }\n    if _bead_watcher is not None:\n        response[\"watcher\"] = _bead_watcher.health_snapshot()\n    else:\n        # Watcher failed to start or not initialized\n        response[\"watcher\"] = {\n            \"alive\": False,\n            \"last_tick_at\": None,\n            \"tick_count\": 0,\n            \"interval\": 0,\n        }\n    return response\n\n\n@app.get(\"/\")\nasync def serve_canvas():\n    \"\"\"\n    Serve the canvas HTML page (Phase 0 minimal surface).\n\n    This is the main entry point for the aide-de-camp interface.\n    \"\"\"\n    return FileResponse(CANVAS_PATH)\n\n\n@app.get(\"/canvas.js\")\nasync def serve_canvas_js():\n    \"\"\"Serve the canvas render helpers (createTopicCard + friends).\n\n    Loaded by index.html via ``<script src=\"/canvas.js\">``. Kept as a separate\n    module so the rendering logic is unit-testable headlessly\n    (``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js``) without spinning up a browser.\n    \"\"\"\n    return FileResponse(CANVAS_JS_PATH, media_type=\"application/javascript\")\n\n\n@app.websocket(\"/voice\")\nasync def voice_session(websocket: WebSocket):\n    \"\"\"\n    Voice session endpoint using OpenAI Realtime API.\n\n    Handles:\n    - WebRTC connection establishment\n    - Tool-as-trigger dispatch_intent calls\n    - Async result delivery for narration\n    - Surface switch events (audio-to-canvas continuity)\n    \"\"\"\n    await websocket.accept()\n\n    # Get session ID from query param or create new\n    session_id = websocket.query_params.get(\"session_id\") or str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    logger.info(f\"Voice session connecting: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Get API key from environment\n    api_key = os.getenv(\"OPENAI_API_KEY\")\n    if not api_key:\n        await websocket.send_json({\n            \"type\": \"error\",\n            \"error\": \"OpenAI API key not configured\"\n        })\n        await websocket.close(code=1011, reason=\"API key missing\")\n        return\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register audio surface\n    audio_surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"audio\")\n    surface_id = audio_surface_id\n    logger.info(f\"Registered audio surface: {surface_id}\")\n\n    # Load voice prompt\n    voice_prompt = load_voice_prompt(VOICE_PROMPT_PATH)\n\n    # Surface switch handler for audio-to-canvas continuity\n    async def on_surface_switch(surface_type: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle surface switch event (audio -> canvas).\"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Surface switch: {surface_type}\")\n        try:\n            result = await handle_surface_switch(surface_type, session_id, voice)\n            # Send acknowledgment to client\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"adc.surface_switch_ack\",\n                \"surface\": surface_type,\n                \"pending_count\": len(result[\"pending_results\"]),\n                \"summary\": result[\"catch_up_summary\"],\n            })\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Surface switch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n\n    # Memory extraction handler for turn completion\n    memory_handler = create_memory_handler(session_id=session_id, api_key=api_key)\n    if memory_handler:\n        logger.info(f\"Memory extraction enabled for session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Create voice session\n    voice = VoiceSession(\n        websocket=websocket,\n        model=DEFAULT_MODEL,\n        api_key=api_key,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        system_message=voice_prompt,\n        voice=DEFAULT_VOICE,\n        logger=logger,\n        on_turn_done=memory_handler.on_turn_done if memory_handler else None,\n        on_surface_switch=on_surface_switch,\n    )\n\n    # Start result listener background task\n    listener_task = None\n    try:\n        listener_task = asyncio.create_task(\n            result_listener(session_id, voice, audio_surface_id)\n        )\n\n        # Register dispatch_intent tool\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"dispatch_intent\",\n            description=(\n                \"Dispatch a user utterance to the intent router. \"\n                \"Returns acknowledgment immediately; results arrive asynchronously. \"\n                \"Use this for any utterance that requires system action.\"\n            ),\n            handler=lambda utterance: dispatch_intent(utterance, session_id, voice),\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"utterance\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"description\": \"The user's utterance to route\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"utterance\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Register change_voice tool\n        async def handle_voice_change(voice: str) -> str:\n            return await voice.change_voice(voice)\n\n        voice.register_tool(\n            name=\"change_voice\",\n            description=f\"Change the assistant's voice. Available: {', '.join(AVAILABLE_VOICES)}\",\n            handler=handle_voice_change,\n            parameters={\n                \"type\": \"object\",\n                \"properties\": {\n                    \"voice\": {\n                        \"type\": \"string\",\n                        \"enum\": AVAILABLE_VOICES,\n                        \"description\": \"The voice to switch to\"\n                    }\n                },\n                \"required\": [\"voice\"]\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Run the session\n        await voice.run()\n\n    except WebSocketDisconnect:\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session disconnected: {session_id}\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Voice session error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        try:\n            await websocket.send_json({\n                \"type\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e)\n            })\n        except:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        # Cancel listener task\n        if listener_task:\n            listener_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await listener_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n        # Mark surface disconnected\n        await store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Voice session ended: {session_id}\")\n\n\n@app.post(\"/router\")\nasync def route_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent router endpoint.\n\n    Classifies utterances into intent types and routes to appropriate strands.\n    Task-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads.\n    Other intents are routed to fetch + synthesize strands.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Routing utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Process each routed intent\n        intent_results = []\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n\n            # Process the intent (escalate or fetch+synthesize)\n            result = await router.process_intent(routed_intent)\n            intent_results.append(result)\n\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"intents\": intent_results,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Router error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Router error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/dispatch\")\nasync def dispatch_intent(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Dispatch endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream.\n\n    Phase 0 core query loop:\n    1. Routes utterance to intents via intent router\n    2. For each intent, runs fetch + synthesize strands in parallel\n    3. Streams results via SSE to active canvas\n    4. Returns acknowledgment with intent IDs\n\n    Returns acknowledgment immediately with intent IDs for tracking.\n    Results are streamed via SSE to connected canvas surfaces.\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    logger.info(f\"Dispatching utterance: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    router = get_intent_router(store)\n\n    # Generate utterance ID if not provided\n    if not utterance_id:\n        utterance_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Create utterance record\n    await store.create_utterance(session_id, utterance, utterance_id)\n\n    # Route the utterance\n    try:\n        routed_intents = await router.route_utterance(\n            utterance=utterance,\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        # Create intent records and process in parallel\n        intent_tasks = []\n        intent_ids = []\n\n        for routed_intent in routed_intents:\n            # Create intent record in store\n            classification = routed_intent.classification\n            await store.create_intent(\n                utterance_id=utterance_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            )\n            intent_ids.append(routed_intent.intent_id)\n\n            # Create task for parallel processing\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                router.process_intent(routed_intent),\n                name=f\"process_{routed_intent.intent_id[:8]}\"\n            )\n            intent_tasks.append((routed_intent.intent_id, task))\n\n        # Process intents in parallel and stream results via SSE\n        async def stream_results():\n            \"\"\"Process intents and stream results to SSE.\"\"\"\n            results = []\n\n            for intent_id, task in intent_tasks:\n                try:\n                    result = await task\n                    results.append(result)\n\n                    # Broadcast result_created so canvas reloads topics\n                    if _broadcaster and surface_id:\n                        emit_start = time.monotonic()\n                        # Include rendered card HTML in SSE so canvas injects it directly\n                        # (Component card when matched, fallback when card_fallback=True)\n                        sse_data = {\n                            \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                            \"topic_id\": result.get(\"topic_id\"),\n                            \"summary\": result.get(\"summary\"),\n                            \"urgency\": result.get(\"urgency\"),\n                        }\n                        # Add component_id when available (for canvas tracking)\n                        if result.get(\"component_id\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"component_id\"] = result[\"component_id\"]\n                        # Add card_fallback flag when available (signals client to use fallback)\n                        if result.get(\"card_fallback\") is not None:\n                            sse_data[\"card_fallback\"] = result[\"card_fallback\"]\n\n                        await _broadcaster.broadcast(\n                            SSEEvent(\n                                event_type=\"result_created\",\n                                target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                                data=sse_data,\n                                rendered_html=result.get(\"rendered_html\"),\n                            )\n                        )\n                        # Record the SSE emit cost for this intent thread\n                        # (Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Non-fatal.\n                        try:\n                            await store.record_dispatch_timings(\n                                intent_id,\n                                sse_emit_ms=int((time.monotonic() - emit_start) * 1000),\n                            )\n                        except Exception as te:\n                            logger.warning(f\"sse_emit timing not recorded for {intent_id}: {te}\")\n\n                except Exception as e:\n                    logger.error(f\"Intent processing failed: {e}\")\n                    results.append({\n                        \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                        \"status\": \"error\",\n                        \"error\": str(e),\n                    })\n\n            return results\n\n        # Start parallel processing in background\n        asyncio.create_task(stream_results())\n\n        # Return acknowledgment immediately\n        return {\n            \"utterance_id\": utterance_id,\n            \"session_id\": session_id,\n            \"intent_count\": len(intent_ids),\n            \"intent_ids\": intent_ids,\n            \"status\": \"dispatched\",\n            \"message\": f\"Dispatched {len(intent_ids)} intents for parallel processing\",\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Dispatch error: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Dispatch error: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/timings\")\nasync def report_client_timings(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"Record client-reported dispatch timings for an intent thread.\n\n    The server-side stages (router/fetch/synthesize/escalate/sse_emit_ms) are\n    recorded by the router and the /dispatch endpoint; this endpoint is the wire\n    for the two client-reported stages in the latency budget — STT final\n    transcript (stt_ms) and first card render (first_render_ms) — which only the\n    client can measure (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md).\n    Both are nullable: a dispatch with no client reporter (the text box, the\n    /api/v1/test/dispatch harness) simply never calls this and those columns\n    stay NULL.\n\n    Body: {\"intent_id\": \"...\", \"stt_ms\": 312, \"first_render_ms\": 90}\n    Either timing field is optional; intent_id is required.\n    \"\"\"\n    intent_id = request.get(\"intent_id\")\n    if not intent_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"intent_id is required\"},\n        )\n\n    fields: dict[str, int] = {}\n    for name in (\"stt_ms\", \"first_render_ms\"):\n        value = request.get(name)\n        if value is not None:\n            fields[name] = int(value)\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    try:\n        # Upserts into the existing server-written row (created_at unchanged);\n        # if no server row exists yet this still creates one keyed by the\n        # client-reported intent_id so the timing is never lost.\n        await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **fields)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"client timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Failed to record timings: {e}\"},\n        )\n\n    return {\"ok\": True, \"intent_id\": intent_id, \"recorded\": list(fields.keys())}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/timings/percentiles\")\nasync def get_latency_percentiles_endpoint(since: int | None = None):\n    \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per dispatch stage (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n\n    Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n    that has at least one captured sample. The latency-baseline bead consumes\n    the un-windowed store helper directly; this endpoint exposes the same\n    numbers over HTTP for the Phase 5 rehearsal checklist and on-demand\n    inspection. Optional ``since`` (unix timestamp) windows to recent\n    dispatches only.\n    \"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n    return await store.get_latency_percentiles(since=since)\n\n\n@app.post(\"/escalate\")\nasync def escalate_endpoint(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Escalate an intent to a NEEDLE bead for durable async handling.\n\n    For task-profile intents that require tracking and async execution:\n    - Formulates bead body via LLM\n    - Creates bead via bf CLI\n    - Returns pending-card spec\n    \"\"\"\n    utterance = request.get(\"utterance\", \"\")\n    utterance_id = request.get(\"utterance_id\")\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    intent_type = request.get(\"intent_type\", \"task-profile\")\n    project_slug = request.get(\"project_slug\")\n    topic_id = request.get(\"topic_id\")\n    metadata = request.get(\"metadata\", {})\n    context = request.get(\"context\", {})\n\n    logger.info(f\"Escalating intent for session {session_id}: {utterance[:100]}...\")\n\n    # Create escalate request\n    escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n        intent_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),\n        session_id=session_id,\n        utterance=utterance,\n        intent_type=intent_type,\n        project_slug=project_slug,\n        topic_id=topic_id,\n        context=context,\n        metadata=metadata,\n    )\n\n    # Store the intent\n    store = await get_store()\n    if utterance_id:\n        intent_id = await store.create_intent(\n            utterance_id=utterance_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            project_slug=project_slug,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        escalate_request.intent_id = intent_id\n\n    try:\n        # Escalate to bead\n        result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n        logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {escalate_request.intent_id} to bead {result.bead_id}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": result.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n            \"status\": result.status,\n            \"pending_card\": result.pending_card,\n        }\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=500,\n            content={\"error\": f\"Escalate failed: {str(e)}\"}\n        )\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/environment\")\nasync def get_environment():\n    \"\"\"Return the discovered environment: all git repos and bead workspaces across all hosts.\"\"\"\n    from .environment.discovery import get_registry\n    import time\n    registry = get_registry()\n    if not registry:\n        return {\"error\": \"Registry not yet initialized\"}\n    entries = [\n        {\n            \"name\": e.name,\n            \"slug\": e.slug,\n            \"path\": str(e.path),\n            \"host\": e.host,\n            \"display_path\": e.display_path,\n            \"has_beads\": e.has_beads,\n            \"remote_url\": e.remote_url,\n            \"remote_name\": e.remote_name,\n        }\n        for e in sorted(registry.all_entries(), key=lambda x: (x.host or \"\", x.slug))\n    ]\n    summary = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"total_repos\": summary[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": summary[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": summary[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": summary[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": summary[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"last_scan_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n        \"repos\": entries,\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/api/v1/registry\")\nasync def get_registry_endpoint():\n    \"\"\"Return the merged project registry (config/registry.yaml + discovery).\n\n    This is the DB-independent data source for the first-run welcome card (the\n    built-in family #2 — see plan, Component Library → Built-in cards, and Cold\n    start & demo seed). The welcome card renders even against an empty\n    components.db because the project list comes from this YAML-backed registry,\n    not the component DB. Returns only the welcome-relevant fields per project\n    (slug, name, description, intent_support, aliases) so no internal paths leak\n    to the served frontend.\n    \"\"\"\n    reg = get_yaml_registry()\n    projects = []\n    for slug, entry in (reg.get(\"projects\") or {}).items():\n        projects.append({\n            \"slug\": slug,\n            \"name\": slug,\n            \"description\": entry.get(\"description\") or \"\",\n            \"intent_support\": entry.get(\"intent_support\") or [],\n            \"aliases\": entry.get(\"aliases\") or [],\n        })\n    return {\"projects\": projects}\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/environment/refresh\")\nasync def trigger_environment_refresh():\n    \"\"\"Trigger an immediate environment rescan to discover new repos.\"\"\"\n    import time\n    registry = await refresh_registry()\n    s = registry.summary()\n    return {\n        \"status\": \"refreshed\",\n        \"total_repos\": s[\"total_repos\"],\n        \"local_repos\": s[\"local_repos\"],\n        \"remote_repos\": s[\"remote_repos\"],\n        \"remote_hosts\": s[\"remote_hosts\"],\n        \"beaded_repos\": s[\"beaded_repos\"],\n        \"scanned_at\": get_last_scan_at(),\n    }\n\n\n@app.get(\"/events\")\nasync def canvas_sse(\n    session_id: str,\n    surface_id: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for:\n    - Topic changes\n    - New results\n    - Intent status updates\n    - Workload summaries\n\n    Sends workload summary on connect for reconnection support.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n        logger.info(f\"Registered canvas surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=\"canvas\",\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event with workload summary\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",  # Disable nginx buffering\n        },\n    )\n\n\n@app.post(\"/heartbeat\")\nasync def heartbeat(request: dict):\n    \"\"\"\n    Heartbeat endpoint for canvas to update surface last_seen.\n\n    Called periodically by the canvas to prevent surface from being marked idle.\n    \"\"\"\n    session_id = request.get(\"session_id\")\n    surface_id = request.get(\"surface_id\")\n\n    if not session_id or not surface_id:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=400,\n            content={\"error\": \"Missing session_id or surface_id\"}\n        )\n\n    store = await get_store()\n    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@app.get(\"/topics\")\nasync def get_topics(session_id: str):\n    \"\"\"\n    Get active topic cards for a session.\n\n    Returns topics with staleness info for canvas rendering.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _topic_manager:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Topic manager not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n    return {\n        \"topics\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n    }\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# API v1 Endpoints\n# surface_type: str\n\n\nclass HeartbeatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n\n\nclass FeedbackRequestModel(BaseModel):\n    feedback: str\n    feedback_type: str = endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/register\")\nasync def api_v1_register_surface(request: SurfaceRegisterRequest):\n    \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n    store = await get_store()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    session = await store.get_session(request.session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n    else:\n        session_id = request.session_id\n\n    # Register surface\n    surface_id = await store.register_surface(\n        session_id,\n        request.surface_type\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"session_id\": session_id\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat\")\nasync def session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n    if not session:\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        logger.info(f\"Created new session: {session_id}\")\n\n    # Register or update surface\n    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        logger.info(f\"Registered {surface_type} surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n\n    # Create SSE connection\n    connection = broadcaster.register(\n        surface_id=surface_id,\n        session_id=session_id,\n        surface_type=surface_type,\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"surface_id\": surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n            })\n\n            yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n        headers={\n            \"Cache-Control\": \"no-cache\",\n            \"Connection\": \"keep-alive\",\n            \"X-Accel-Buffering\": \"no\",\n        },\n    )\n\n\n# Feedback and Self-Modification endpoints\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback\")\nasync def api_v1_process_feedback(request: FeedbackRequestModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Process user feedback for self-modification or component iteration.\n\n    Returns a diff for approval or confirmation of application.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not _feedback_processor:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Feedback processor not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    # Map string to enum\n    feedback_type_map = {\n        \"self_modification\": FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n        \"component_iteration\": FeedbackType.COMPONENT_ITERATION,\n        \"routing_correction\": FeedbackType.ROUTING_CORRECTION,\n        \"behavior_adjustment\": FeedbackType.BEHAVIOR_ADJUSTMENT,\n    }\n\n    feedback_type = feedback_type_map.get(\n        request.feedback_type,\n        FeedbackType.SELF_MODIFICATION\n    )\n\n    feedback_req = FeedbackRequest(\n        feedback=request.feedback,\n        feedback_type=feedback_type,\n        context=request.context,\n        session_id=request.session_id,\n        require_approval=request.require_approval\n    )\n\n    response = await _feedback_processor.process_feedback(feedback_req)\n\n    return {\n        \"status\": response.status,\n        \"message\": response.message,\n        \"confidence\": response.confidence,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n        \"artifact_type\": response.artifact_type.value if response.artifact_type else None,\n        \"approval_id\": getattr(response, 'approval_id', None),\n        \"diff\": {\n            \"artifact_name\": response.diff.artifact_name,\n            \"artifact_type\": response.diff.artifact_type.value,\n            \"change_summary\": response.diff.change_summary,\n            \"confidence\": response.diff.confidence,\n            # Don't include full before/after in response to save space\n            # Client can fetch separately if needed\n        } if response.diff else None\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/approve\")\nasync def api_v1_approve_change(request: ApprovalRequest):\n    \"\"\"Approve response.message,\n        \"artifact_name\": response.artifact_name,\n    }\n\n\n@app.post(\"/api/v1/feedback/reject\")\nasync def api_v1_reject_change(request: ApprovalRequest, reason: Optional[str]   if not _component_library:\n        return JSONResponse(\n            status_code=503,\n            content={\"error\": \"Component library not initialized\"}\n        )\n\n    updated = prompt first\n        content = _reload_manager.get_prompt(artifact_name)\n        artifact_type = \"prompt\"\n    except KeyError:\n        try:\n            # Try as config\n            content = content={\"error\": \"Artifact not found\"}\n            )\n\n    return {\n        \"name\": artifact_name,\n        \"type\": artifact_type,\n        \"content\": content if artifact_type ==   return b\n75\t\n76\t\n77\tasync def _collect_sse_events(broadcaster, conn, wanted_types, *, timeout=2.0):\n78\t    \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive (excluding tests for component and fallback card rendering via SSE (bead adc-35eq).\n\nThese tests verify that both render paths work correctly through the full\ndispatch → SSE → render pipeline:\n\n1. get_broadcaster()\n    if not getattr(b, \"_running\", False):\n        await b.start()\n    return b\n\n\nasync def _collect_sse_events(broadcaster, conn, wanted_types, *, timeout=2.0):\n    \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive (excluding 'connected').\"\"\"\n    wanted = set(wanted_types)\n    collected = []\n\n    async def drain():\n        async for wire in broadcaster.event_generator(conn):\n            for etype, data in parse_sse_stream(wire):\n                if etype == \"connected\":\n                    continue\n                collected.append((etype, data))\n                if wanted <= {e for e, _ in collected}:\n                    return\n\n    task = asyncio.create_task(drain())\n    try:\n        await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n        task.cancel()\n        raise\n    return collected\n\n\n# --- Component path tests (seeded match) Create a session and surface\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n\n        # Create a topic and result with no component match (fallback)\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Novel Shape\",\n            topic_type=\"research\",\n            project_slugs=[],\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        result_id = await store.create_result(\n            intent_id=\"intent-fallback-1\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=\"Novel result shape\",\n            data={\"novel_field\": \"novel_value\", \"another_field\": 42},\n            urgency=\"low\",\n            result_type=\"research:general\",\n        )\n\n        # Update result with fallback flag (simulating no component match)\n        # Note: rendered_html and component_id are sent via SSE, not stored in DB\n        await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, card_fallback=True)\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event\n        b = await _started_broadcaster()\n        conn = b.register(\n            surface_id=surface_id, session_id=session_id, surface_type=\"canvas\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Collect SSE events\n            events = await _collect_sse_events(b, conn, [\"result_created\"])\n            assert len(events) >= 1\n\n            etype, data = events[0]\n            assert etype == \"result_created\"\n            assert \"card_fallback\" in data\n            assert data[\"card_fallback\"] is True\n            assert \"rendered_html\" not in data or data.get(\"rendered_html\") is None\n\n        finally:\n            b.unregister(conn.connection_id)","new_string":"    async def test_no_component_match_includes_rendered_html_in_sse(\n        self, isolated_store\n    ):\n        \"\"\"When no component matches, fallback HTML is included in SSE.\"\"\"\n        from src.render.hot_path import render_fallback_card\n\n        store = isolated_store\n\n        # Create a session and surface\n        session_id = await store.create_session()\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, \"canvas\")\n\n        # Create a topic and result with no component match (fallback)\n        topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n            label=\"Novel Shape\",\n            topic_type=\"research\",\n            project_slugs=[],\n            session_id=session_id,\n        )\n\n        result_id = await store.create_result(\n            intent_id=\"intent-fallback-1\",\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=\"Novel result shape\",\n            data={\"novel_field\": \"novel_value\", \"another_field\": 42},\n            urgency=\"low\",\n            result_type=\"research:general\",\n        )\n\n        # Update result with fallback flag (simulating no component match)\n        # Note: rendered_html and component_id are sent via SSE, not stored in DB\n        await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, card_fallback=True)\n\n        # Generate the fallback HTML server-side (as hot-path renderer does)\n        fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n            summary=\"Novel result shape\",\n            data={\"novel_field\": \"novel_value\", \"another_field\": 42},\n            urgency=\"low\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event\n        b = await _started_broadcaster()\n        conn = b.register(\n            surface_id=surface_id, session_id=session_id, surface_type=\"canvas\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Broadcast the event with fallback rendered_html (as /dispatch does)\n            await b.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=\"result_created\",\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": \"intent-fallback-1\",\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"summary\": \"Novel result shape\",\n                        \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                        \"card_fallback\": True,\n                    },\n                    rendered_html=fallback_html,  # Include fallback HTML\n                    target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            # Collect SSE events\n            events = await _collect_sse_events(b, conn, [\"result_created\"])\n            assert len(events) >= 1\n\n            etype, data = events[0]\n            assert etype == \"result_created\"\n            assert \"card_fallback\" in data\n            assert data[\"card_fallback\"] is True\n            # Verify fallback HTML is included in SSE\n            assert \"rendered_html\" in data\n            assert data[\"rendered_html\"] is not None\n            assert \"fallback-card\" in data[\"rendered_html\"]\n            assert \"novel_field\" in data[\"rendered_html\"]\n            assert \"novel_value\" in data[\"rendered_html\"]\n\n        finally:\n            b.unregister(conn.connection_id)"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b0fd006d-9530-490b-83d9-11b5dcd97f95","uuid":"11967b43-b4d4-41d5-9a4d-7a632cfbbbcb","timestamp":"2026-07-23T15:12:56.858Z"}

_collect_sse_events(broadcaster, conn, wanted_types, *, timeout=2.0):\n    \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive (excluding 'connected').\"\"\"\n    wanted = set(wanted_types)\n    collected = []\n\n    async def drain():\n        async for wire in broadcaster.event_generator(conn):\n            for etype, data in parse_sse_stream(wire):\n                if etype == \"connected\":\n                    continue\n                collected.append((etype, data))\n                if wanted <= {e for e, _ in collected}:\n                    return\n\n    task = asyncio.create_task(drain())\n    try:\n        await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n        task.cancel()\n        raise\n    return collected\n\n\n# ---
